typecheck.details: Clojure is dynamically typed; no separate typecheck phase

project-skeleton.summary: Project skeleton correctly implemented with all 7 dependencies, directory structure matching spec §9, .gitignore, and passing test infrastructure.

taxonomy-macros.summary: Three taxonomy macros with spec validation, atom-backed registries, duplicate rejection, conditional requirements. 24 tests, 54 assertions. Minor: unsafe polarity edge case, symbol/keyword API inconsistency.

taxonomy-registry-queries.summary: All query functions implemented: descendants, families-with-tag, coverage-matrix, missing-coverage, all-* functions, reset!, resolve-transforms with seed-deterministic medium sampling. 31 tests, 553 assertions.

source-transform-definitions.summary: def-source and def-transform macros following established registry pattern. Spec validation, url-or-path enforcement, optional impl. 48 tests.

cross-macro-integration.summary: Five referential integrity checks across all registries. Full §2 taxonomy integration test (13 categories, 13 families, 3 labels, 4 transforms, 2 sources). 19 tests.

nonBlockingIssuesSummary.description: Unsafe polarity validation edge case: [:attack-family :attack-family] passes but doesn't enforce both required keys
API inconsistency: def-attack-family takes bare symbol, def-harm-category/def-intent-label take keywords
resolve-transforms output order non-deterministic across JVM restarts (hash map iteration order)
RNG advancement positions limited to 7 — may cause correlation with >7 medium-affinity transforms
No test for resolve-transforms with empty :transforms map or descendants with unregistered children
Double-optionality in url/path specs (nilable + opt-un) — functionally correct but unusual
Parent↔children bidirectionality not enforced in test fixtures (data modeling gap, not required by spec)

appliedUpdates.description: Documented reset! shadowing pattern, TOCTOU registration note, seed-mixing strategy for resolve-transforms, validation/ vs verification/ namespace distinction, cross-registry validation layer

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: DSL macro argument naming should be standardized — either all macros accept bare symbols (converted to keywords internally) or all accept keywords directly. Currently def-attack-family takes symbols while def-harm-category and def-intent-label take keywords.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: taxonomy-macros review found families.clj:26 uses (keyword (name family-name)) while categories.clj:19 and labels.clj:29 pass keywords directly. This inconsistency may confuse future DSL users.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: (:refer-clojure :exclude [reset!]) is required in all registry namespaces that define their own reset! function. This is now established in 3 registries.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three registries (taxonomy/registry, pipeline/sources, transform/registry) all independently discovered the need for this pattern. taxonomy-macros worker spent debugging time on the shadowing issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify determinism convention: 'All randomness must be seeded' should also address ordering determinism of returned collections. Functions returning vectors/sequences from hash-map iteration should sort results for cross-JVM-session stability.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: taxonomy-registry-queries review found resolve-transforms uses (keys transform-config) which produces non-deterministic ordering across JVM restarts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: clojure-dsl-worker skill: Add note that skeleton/infrastructure features may skip the RED phase of TDD since there's no code to fail against initially.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: project-skeleton worker noted this deviation in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

rejectedObservations.observation: .factory files bundled in feature commits should be separated
rejectedObservations.reason: This only applies to the initial project-skeleton commit where .factory/ infrastructure was bootstrapped alongside the project. Not a recurring pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: atom+spec registry pattern used 3x could benefit from shared base/protocol
rejectedObservations.reason: Premature abstraction. The three registries are similar but have different validation logic and field shapes. Abstracting now would add indirection without clear benefit. Document the pattern instead (done in architecture.md).
